Resume

Experience

Experience

Roles across data engineering, analytics, and operations-facing reporting, with the same basic job each time: make important workflows and numbers dependable.

Data engineering Analytics engineering Product analytics

Fast read

The job has been similar in each role.

The starting point is usually some mix of messy inputs, unclear definitions, or brittle reporting. The work is to turn that into something another team can use without needing constant support.

Data

shared source

Workflow

clean handoffs

Use

clear reporting view

EnvoyX

Sep 2025 - Present

Berkeley, CA

GTM Data Engineer

Built GTM data systems that gave sales, marketing, and product teams a shared customer view and reporting they could trust.

Team view

shared customer view

Working flow

steadier reporting flow

Decision use

cleaner team handoff

Customer identity stitching · Attribution and lifecycle reporting · ELT reliability

  • Pulled CRM, product, billing, and support data into one model so teams could stop working from partial customer records.
  • Reworked attribution and revenue logic so teams could answer the same question from the same source instead of separate spreadsheets.
  • Added tests, monitoring, and reusable build patterns so the reporting stayed stable after handoff.

Project notes

Open the related notes to see the problem, what changed, and why it helped.

Customer 360 and Revenue Attribution Open note

Different teams were working from different customer records, so even simple reporting questions kept turning into reconciliation work.

Professional work

State one customer view
System shared revenue logic
Result easier budget calls

What changed

  • Stitched CRM, GA4, product, billing, and support records into one account-level model.
  • Defined shared identity, touchpoint, conversion, and revenue logic so teams were not reconciling the same customer in different ways.
  • Added dashboards, lineage, and ownership rules so the model stayed useful after handoff.

Why it helped

The value was not a prettier report. The value was giving GTM teams one version of the customer so planning and budget decisions stopped breaking on record mismatch.

Snowflake · dbt · SQL · Salesforce · GA4

See full project note
ELT Reliability and Monitoring Open note

The pipeline worked just enough to be relied on, but not enough to be trusted.

Professional work

State steadier runs
System clearer failure paths
Result faster new-source setup

What changed

  • Built a layered ELT flow with staging models, curated marts, and reusable onboarding patterns for new sources.
  • Added dbt tests, freshness checks, lineage docs, and monitoring so reliability could be checked directly instead of guessed at.
  • Used CI workflows and data contracts to catch bad changes before they reached dashboards or leadership reporting.

Why it helped

Once the pipeline had tests, runbooks, and visible ownership, analysts could stop treating every table like a special case.

Python · SQL · dbt · Airflow · GitHub Actions

See full project note

Related work

Customer 360 and Revenue Attribution · ELT Reliability and Monitoring

Tools

Snowflake · dbt · Airflow · SQL · Python · GA4 · Salesforce

United Parcel Service

Jun 2025 - Sep 2025

Berkeley, CA

Data Analyst Intern

Built KPI and reporting systems that replaced manual updates with a clearer operating view for logistics teams.

Team view

one KPI language

Working flow

less manual work

Decision use

clearer weekly reviews

KPI governance · Self-serve operational BI · Dashboard performance tuning

  • Moved teams onto one set of KPI definitions so meetings spent less time debating the number itself.
  • Built dashboards and QA checks that made weekly reporting easier for leaders to read and easier for analysts to maintain.
  • Tightened the model and refresh flow so updates landed more reliably and with less manual cleanup.

Project notes

Open the related notes to see the problem, what changed, and why it helped.

Shared KPI Layer for Product and Ops Open note

Leadership meetings kept stalling because each team had its own version of the same KPI.

Professional work

State one KPI language
System cleaner weekly reviews
Result less reporting churn

What changed

  • Unified event, warehouse, and reporting logic behind a governed metric layer instead of dashboard-specific calculations.
  • Added QA checks, ownership metadata, and freshness monitoring so executive metrics behaved like maintained products.
  • Supported drill-down workflows for operators and leaders instead of a single static dashboard.

Why it helped

The dashboard only became useful once the definitions settled down. After that, the meeting could move from reconciliation to decision.

SQL · dbt · Power BI · Azure SQL · QA monitoring

See full project note
BizOps Control Tower Open note

Important updates were spread across decks, sheets, and team dashboards, so blockers stayed hidden too long.

Professional work

State clearer priorities
System faster leadership prep
Result better view of blockers

What changed

  • Combined initiative tracking, KPI monitoring, and bottleneck views into one reporting surface.
  • Built drill-down views and weekly review packs so leadership could move from status collection to prioritization.
  • Made trade-offs explicit by tying project health back to shared business measures instead of isolated team updates.

Why it helped

The key shift was simple: the review stopped being a recap and started acting like a working control room.

SQL · Power BI · Excel · Operational reporting · KPI design

See full project note

Related work

Shared KPI Layer for Product and Ops · BizOps Control Tower

Tools

Power BI · Azure SQL · DAX · Power Query · SQL

United Parcel Service

Apr 2023 - Dec 2024

Vancouver, BC

Brokerage Data Administrator

Worked on brokerage intake and review flows where messy documents had to become clean, review-ready records.

Team view

cleaner intake

Working flow

clearer review queue

Decision use

fewer avoidable handoffs

OCR-assisted document intake · Exception routing and QA · Compliance visibility

  • Helped turn OCR-assisted document intake into records that people could actually review and use.
  • Added clearer validation rules, backlog visibility, and escalation paths for higher-risk cases.
  • Tightened the review steps and record structure so the workflow was easier to run day to day.

Project notes

Open the related notes to see the problem, what changed, and why it helped.

ELT Reliability and Monitoring Open note

The pipeline worked just enough to be relied on, but not enough to be trusted.

Professional work

State steadier runs
System clearer failure paths
Result faster new-source setup

What changed

  • Built a layered ELT flow with staging models, curated marts, and reusable onboarding patterns for new sources.
  • Added dbt tests, freshness checks, lineage docs, and monitoring so reliability could be checked directly instead of guessed at.
  • Used CI workflows and data contracts to catch bad changes before they reached dashboards or leadership reporting.

Why it helped

Once the pipeline had tests, runbooks, and visible ownership, analysts could stop treating every table like a special case.

Python · SQL · dbt · Airflow · GitHub Actions

See full project note
Compliance Review Operations Open note

The review queue was hard to read, which made delays and audit follow-up harder than they needed to be.

Professional work

State visible review queue
System cleaner status language
Result faster audit follow-up

What changed

  • Centralized intake, inventory, review status, and evidence tracking into one operating view.
  • Added structured status language, aging logic, and escalation alerts so SLA risk became visible earlier.
  • Organized artifacts into a searchable repository that made audits and follow-up easier.

Why it helped

The project worked because it made the queue easy to read. Once the workflow had clear status language and a shared structure, leadership could manage risk with less guesswork.

SQL · Power BI · Workflow tooling · Documentation · Reporting

See full project note

Related work

ELT Reliability and Monitoring · Compliance Review Operations

Tools

Python · Tesseract OCR · Structured QA · Operational reporting

Zego

Jun 2022 - Aug 2022

San Diego, CA

Business Analyst Intern, Product Analytics

Built product analytics workflows that helped teams understand onboarding, read experiments, and spot retention risk earlier.

Team view

clearer onboarding story

Working flow

better test readouts

Decision use

earlier retention signal

Funnel and cohort analysis · Experiment measurement · Retention modeling

  • Built reusable funnel views and experiment readouts so product teams could see where onboarding was breaking.
  • Connected churn scoring to follow-up actions instead of leaving it as a model output with no clear owner.
  • Cleaned up the refresh logic and reporting flow so teams had a steadier read on what was happening.

Project notes

Open the related notes to see the problem, what changed, and why it helped.

Onboarding Funnel and Experiment Readouts Open note

Changes were shipping faster than the team could agree on what was working.

Professional work

State clearer drop-off points
System cleaner test readouts
Result faster product calls

What changed

  • Standardized event taxonomy, exposure logging, and reusable funnel models across onboarding and checkout flows.
  • Built experiment readout templates with guardrail metrics so results were easier to compare from one launch to the next.
  • Added cohort and decision-tracking views that connected analysis back to the change a team actually made.

Why it helped

The project mattered because it turned experiments into a repeatable workflow. Teams could see the change, read the result, and make the next call faster.

Snowflake · SQL · Python · Experimentation · Dashboarding

See full project note
Churn and Retention Early Warning Open note

A churn score by itself was not useful enough. The team still needed a clear order of action and a way to know when the model had gone stale.

Professional work

State earlier risk signal
System clearer outreach order
Result model stays useful longer

What changed

  • Combined behavioral, billing, and support features into a ranked risk model for at-risk accounts.
  • Focused evaluation on actionability with calibration checks and intervention-aware thresholds.
  • Added CRM integration, drift checks, and outcome feedback so the model stayed useful after launch.

Why it helped

The model only became valuable once it helped a team decide who to contact first and when the scoring needed another look.

Python · SQL · scikit-learn · Snowflake · Monitoring

See full project note

Related work

Onboarding Funnel and Experiment Readouts · Churn and Retention Early Warning

Tools

Snowflake SQL · Experimentation · PyTorch · Python · Product analytics

Crosswork.us

Aug 2022 - Dec 2022

Remote

Venture Capital Intern

Used scoring, lightweight experimentation, and structured research to make sourcing and early commercial decisions easier to work through.

Team view

stronger lead lists

Working flow

faster team reviews

Decision use

clearer commercial choices

Scoring and prioritization · Commercial experimentation · Executive synthesis

  • Automated prospect building and ranking so outreach started from a better list instead of a rough first pass.
  • Turned fuzzy commercial questions into research and reporting that leadership could use in real discussions.
  • {"Used simple analytics where it helped most" => "making the next decision clearer and the follow-up stronger."}

Project notes

Open the related notes to see the problem, what changed, and why it helped.

BizOps Control Tower Open note

Important updates were spread across decks, sheets, and team dashboards, so blockers stayed hidden too long.

Professional work

State clearer priorities
System faster leadership prep
Result better view of blockers

What changed

  • Combined initiative tracking, KPI monitoring, and bottleneck views into one reporting surface.
  • Built drill-down views and weekly review packs so leadership could move from status collection to prioritization.
  • Made trade-offs explicit by tying project health back to shared business measures instead of isolated team updates.

Why it helped

The key shift was simple: the review stopped being a recap and started acting like a working control room.

SQL · Power BI · Excel · Operational reporting · KPI design

See full project note
Pricing Scenario Workbench Open note

The team needed a better way to compare pricing paths without arguing from instinct alone.

Professional work

State clearer pricing trade-offs
System same-day scenario review
Result more trustworthy rollout checks

What changed

  • Modeled historical price-demand behavior with business-rule constraints such as margin floors and segment-level trade-offs.
  • Built a scenario engine and dashboard that let operators compare candidate prices instead of accepting one opaque answer.
  • Tracked realized versus predicted lift so the model could earn trust over time.

Why it helped

The useful part was transparency. People could see the assumptions, compare paths, and check whether the recommendation held up after rollout.

Python · SQL · Optimization logic · Power BI · Scenario modeling

See full project note

Related work

BizOps Control Tower · Pricing Scenario Workbench

Tools

Python · Pandas · A/B testing · Research synthesis

Edwin Tse

I build data systems and reporting workflows that make day-to-day work easier to read, trust, and use.

Outside work I keep coming back to transit systems, public infrastructure, fairness questions, and small automation tools that remove repeat friction.
